symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe unilateral loin-to-groin colicky pain radiating to the testicle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loin-to-groin colicky radiation follows the T10-L1 ureteric referred pattern — highly characteristic of ureteric stone.
AAA can radiate flank-to-groin identically — the overlap is exactly why AAA must be excluded.
Testicular radiation mandates a direct question about the testis itself.

symptoms.symptom_name: Extreme restlessness — unable to lie still, pacing and writhing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colic sufferers characteristically cannot keep still, in contrast to peritonitis.
Peritoneal inflammation makes patients lie perfectly still; restlessness argues against appendicitis.
Stone abrasion of the urothelium commonly produces visible or dipstick haematuria.
Macroscopic haematuria is not typical of appendicitis and points to the urinary tract.
Autonomic (coeliac plexus) stimulation from acute obstruction causes nausea/vomiting; also precludes reliable oral drug absorption.
A distal ureteric stone near the vesico-ureteric junction irritates the bladder, causing dysuria and urgency.
Lower urinary tract symptoms overlap with urinary infection; fever status discriminates.
Absence of fever/rigors argues against pyelonephritis and against an infected obstructed kidney at this moment.
Fever/rigors with obstruction defines the urological emergency; its absence is what makes conservative disposition possible.

symptoms.symptom_name: No syncope, collapse or tearing pain; no known aneurysm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of syncope/collapse/tearing pain lowers AAA probability but does not exclude it without imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oral NSAID (ibuprofen) already taken with no relief

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failed oral analgesia is consistent with severe colic and, clinically, drives the need for parenteral analgesia in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor and diaphoresis during pain spikes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic surge with severe visceral pain; video-observable and patient-reportable, so admissible in telehealth as unverified.
Loin tenderness supports upper urinary tract pathology — a clinician manoeuvre the AI Doctor cannot perform; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of peritonism, rebound or abdominal rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No peritoneal signs point away from a peritonitic surgical abdomen toward an extra-peritoneal (ureteric) process — a clinician exam finding, sealed.
Sympathetic pain response rather than a primary cardiovascular problem; clinician-measured vitals, sealed — the AI infers distress from behaviour, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-contrast CT KUB — radio-opaque right ureteric calculus with proximal hydroureter

investigation_details.expected_result: Radio-opaque calculus within the right ureter with proximal hydroureter/hydronephrosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Stone unconfirmed and AAA not excluded — the diagnosis remains presumptive.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT KUB demonstrating the obstructing calculus is the confirmatory test — never obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Radiating unilateral flank pain + extreme restlessness + macroscopic haematuria + calculus on CT KUB is the classic renal-colic constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line imaging to confirm the stone (size/site), assess obstruction, and exclude AAA and other pathology on a first presentation.
Confirms haematuria and screens for infection (nitrites/leucocytes) — an infected obstructed kidney changes the disposition to emergency decompression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes, urea and creatinine (renal function)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses for post-renal acute kidney injury, especially relevant if a solitary kidney or bilateral obstruction is suspected.
Screens for infection/inflammation supporting urosepsis or an alternative diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Contrast-enhanced CT as the initial study for uncomplicated colic
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Non-contrast CT KUB is the standard for suspected stone; routine contrast is unnecessary and adds contrast risk — the AI Doctor should not over-order it.
